text: "Two-thirds of him is god, one-third of him is human. The Great Goddess [Aruru] designed(?) the model for his body, she prepared his form." (Tablet I)
"Offspring of Lugalbanda, Gilgamesh is strong to perfection, son of the august cow, Rimat-Ninsun." (Tablet I)
"You who were created from the flesh of gods and mankind." (Tablet X -- Utanapishtim to Gilgamesh)

"It was you, Aruru, who created mankind(?), now create a zikru to it/him. Let him be equal to his stormy heart, let them be a match for each other so that Uruk may find peace!" (Tablet I -- gods to Aruru)
"Aruru washed her hands, she pinched off some clay, and threw it into the wilderness. In the wilderness she created valiant Enkidu, born of Silence, endowed with strength by Ninurta." (Tablet I)

"His whole body was shaggy with hair, he had a full head of hair like a woman, his locks billowed in profusion like Ashnan. He knew neither people nor settled living, but wore a garment like Sumukan. He ate grasses with the gazelles, and jostled at the watering hole with the animals." (Tablet I)

"Then they saw the Cedar Mountain, the Dwelling of the Gods, the throne dais of Irnini. Across the face of the mountain the Cedar brought forth luxurious foliage, its shade was good, extremely pleasant." (Tablet V)
"Where Humbaba would walk there was a trail, the roads led straight on, the path was excellent." (Tablet V)
"He has not put on his seven coats of armor... he is wearing only one, but has taken off six." (Tablet IV -- Shamash's tactical intelligence)
"Humbaba's face keeps changing!" (Tablet V -- Gilgamesh)

"Shamash raised up against Humbaba mighty tempests -- Southwind, Northwind, Eastwind, Westwind, Whistling Wind, Piercing Wind, Blizzard, Bad Wind, Wind of Simurru, Demon Wind, Ice Wind, Storm, Sandstorm -- thirteen winds rose up against him and covered Humbaba's face." (Tablet V)
"Ishtar spoke to her father, Anu, saying: 'Father, give me the Bull of Heaven so he can kill Gilgamesh in his dwelling.'" (Tablet VI)
"Anu listened to her statement, and let the Bull of Heaven go down to the earth." (Tablet VI)

"The Bull of Heaven snorted and at his first snort a huge pit opened up, and 100 Young Men of Uruk fell in. At his second snort a huge pit opened up, and 200 Young Men of Uruk fell in. At his third snort a huge pit opened and Enkidu fell in up to his waist." (Tablet VI)

"The hearts of the Great Gods moved them to inflict the Flood. Their Father Anu uttered the oath of secrecy, Valiant Enlil was their Adviser, Ninurta was their Chamberlain, Ennugi was their Minister of Canals." (Tablet XI)

"Ea, the Clever Prince, was under oath with them so he repeated their talk to the reed house: 'Reed house, reed house! Wall, wall! Tear down the house and build a boat! Abandon wealth and seek living beings!'" (Tablet XI)
Vessel specifications: "Its dimensions must measure equal to each other: its length must correspond to its width. Roof it over like the Apsu." (Tablet XI)

Construction: "Its walls were each 10 times 12 cubits in height, the sides of its top were of equal length, 10 times 12 cubits each. I provided it with six decks, thus dividing it into seven levels. The inside of it I divided into nine compartments. I drove plugs to keep out water in its middle part." (Tablet XI)
Materials: "Three times 3,600 units of raw bitumen I poured into the bitumen kiln, three times 3,600 units of pitch." (Tablet XI)

The storm: "The Anunnaki lifted up the torches, setting the land ablaze with their flare. Stunned shock over Adad's deeds overtook the heavens, and turned to blackness all that had been light." (Tablet XI)
"The gods were frightened by the Flood, and retreated, ascending to the heaven of Anu." (Tablet XI)
Aftermath: "Six days and seven nights came the wind and flood, the storm flattening the land... all the human beings had turned to clay! The terrain was as flat as a roof." (Tablet XI)
Ea to Enlil: "It was not I who revealed the secret of the Great Gods, I only made a dream appear to Atrahasis, and thus he heard the secret of the gods." (Tablet XI)
"There is a plant... like a boxthorn, whose thorns will prick your hand like a rose. If your hands reach that plant you will become a young man again." (Tablet XI -- Utanapishtim to Gilgamesh)
"Gilgamesh opened a conduit to the Apsu and attached heavy stones to his feet. They dragged him down, to the Apsu they pulled him. He took the plant, though it pricked his hand." (Tablet XI)

"'This plant is a plant against decay by which a man can attain his survival. I will bring it to Uruk-Haven, and have an old man eat the plant to test it. The plant's name is "The Old Man Becomes a Young Man."'" (Tablet XI)
"A snake smelled the fragrance of the plant, silently came up and carried off the plant. While going back it sloughed off its casing." (Tablet XI)
